documentation.text:
@dev Contract module which provides a basic access control mechanism, where
 there is an account (an owner) that can be granted exclusive access to
 specific functions.
 The initial owner is set to the address provided by the deployer. This can
 later be changed with {transferOwnership}.
 This module is used through inheritance. It will make available the modifier
 `onlyOwner`, which can be applied to your functions to restrict their use to
 the owner.
@dev The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.
@dev The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)
@dev Initializes the contract setting the address provided by the deployer as the initial owner.
@dev Throws if called by any account other than the owner.
@dev Returns the address of the current owner.
@dev Throws if the sender is not the owner.

@dev Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call
 `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner.
 NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner,
 thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Can only be called by the current owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Internal function without access restriction.

@dev Provides information about the current execution context, including the
 sender of the transaction and its data. While these are generally available
 via msg.sender and msg.data, they should not be accessed in such a direct
 manner, since when dealing with meta-transactions the account sending and
 paying for execution may not be the actual sender (as far as an application
 is concerned).
 This contract is only required for intermediate, library-like contracts.

@dev Contract module that helps prevent reentrant calls to a function.
 Inheriting from `ReentrancyGuard` will make the {nonReentrant} modifier
 available, which can be applied to functions to make sure there are no nested
 (reentrant) calls to them.
 Note that because there is a single `nonReentrant` guard, functions marked as
 `nonReentrant` may not call one another. This can be worked around by making
 those functions `private`, and then adding `external` `nonReentrant` entry
 points to them.
 TIP: If EIP-1153 (transient storage) is available on the chain you're deploying at,
 consider using {ReentrancyGuardTransient} instead.
 TIP: If you would like to learn more about reentrancy and alternative ways
 to protect against it, check out our blog post
 https://blog.openzeppelin.com/reentrancy-after-istanbul/[Reentrancy After Istanbul].
@dev Unauthorized reentrant call.

@dev Prevents a contract from calling itself, directly or indirectly.
 Calling a `nonReentrant` function from another `nonReentrant`
 function is not supported. It is possible to prevent this from happening
 by making the `nonReentrant` function external, and making it call a
 `private` function that does the actual work.

@dev Returns true if the reentrancy guard is currently set to "entered", which indicates there is a
 `nonReentrant` function in the call stack.
@notice Length of an uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (0x04 || x || y).
@notice Minimum registration deposit in wei (anti-Sybil).
@notice Cooldown period after deactivation before deposit can be withdrawn.
@notice Maximum value for the reputation score (basis-point scale, 10000 = 100%).
@notice deviceId => DeviceInfo. deviceId = keccak256(pubkey).
@notice Addresses authorized to call updateReputation (e.g. PoACVerifier, BountyMarket).
@notice Total number of registered devices (including deactivated).
@notice Emitted when a new device is registered.
@notice Emitted when a device is deactivated by its owner.
@notice Emitted when a device's reputation score is updated.
@notice Emitted when deposit is withdrawn after deactivation + cooldown.
@dev Restricts access to authorized reputation updaters or the contract owner.
@param _minimumDeposit Initial minimum deposit in wei for device registration.

@notice Update the minimum registration deposit. Owner-only.
 @param _newMinimum New minimum deposit in wei.

@notice Withdraw deposit after deactivation cooldown has elapsed.
 @param _deviceId The deactivated device whose deposit to withdraw.

@dev Internal reputation computation.
      Formula (simple linear model with diminishing returns on volume):
        - verifiedBase  = min(verifiedCount, 10000) (cap raw PoAC contribution)
        - corrobBonus   = corroborations * 2
        - disputePenalty = disputes * 10
        - rawScore = verifiedBase + corrobBonus - disputePenalty
        - Normalize into [0, MAX_REPUTATION] using a logistic-style clamp:
          score = rawScore * MAX_REPUTATION / (rawScore + 1000)
          (Approaches MAX_REPUTATION asymptotically as rawScore grows)
        - If rawScore <= 0, score = 0.

@title PoACVerifier
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice On-chain verification of Proof of Autonomous Cognition (PoAC) records
         submitted by VAPI devices on the IoTeX blockchain.
 @dev Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the
      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.
      Pipeline:
        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).
        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.
        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).
        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest
           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.
        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.
        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.
        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.
           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),
           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.
        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.
      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):
        Offset  Field                  Size
        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B
        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B
        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B
        0x60    world_model_hash       32B
        0x80    inference_result       1B
        0x81    action_code            1B
        0x82    confidence             1B
        0x83    battery_pct            1B
        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)
        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        ---     total                  164B
      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)
      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes
      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.
@notice IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address.
@notice Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature).
@notice Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s).
@notice Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds).
@notice Chain tracking state for each device.
@notice Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract.
@notice Per-device chain integrity state. deviceId => ChainState.
@notice Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain.
@notice Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices.

@notice Sensor commitment schema version for records verified via verifyPoACWithSchema().
         0 = not set / unknown (record submitted via legacy verifyPoAC()).
         1 = v1 environmental (Pebble Tracker: temp/humidity/pressure/GPS/accel).
         2 = v2 kinematic/haptic (DualShock: sticks/triggers/gyro/accel + adaptive trigger).
@notice Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified.
@notice Emitted when a batch verification completes.

@param _deviceRegistry   Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.
 @param _maxTimestampSkew  Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds.
@notice External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Not for direct use.
@dev Parsed fields from the raw body, packed into a struct to avoid stack-too-deep.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes calldata) pure returns (struct PoACVerifier.ParsedFields memory)
function (bytes32) view external returns (struct DeviceRegistry.DeviceInfo memory)

documentation.text:
@title ProgressAttestation
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Verifiable progress proofs for autonomous gaming coaching.
 @dev Stores cryptographic attestations of measurable skill improvement,
      backed by verified PoAC records. Coaching platforms can query these
      proofs to verify student improvement before issuing certificates,
      badges, or processing refund guarantees.
      Workflow:
        1. Student plays with VAPI device, generating PoAC chain
        2. Agent detects improvement (e.g., reaction_time decreased 15%)
        3. Coach or student calls attestProgress() with baseline + current records
        4. Contract verifies both records exist on-chain
        5. Progress attestation stored with metric type and improvement BPS
        6. Coaching platform queries getProgressHistory() for verification
      Metric types:
        REACTION_TIME   — Average reaction time improvement
        ACCURACY        — Stick/aim precision improvement
        CONSISTENCY     — Reduced timing variance
        COMBO_EXECUTION — Complex input sequence mastery
@notice Global attestation counter (also serves as attestation ID).
@notice attestationId => ProgressRecord
@notice deviceId => array of attestation IDs
@notice Prevent duplicate attestations for same baseline+current pair

@notice Thrown when baseline and current records were verified with incompatible schemas.
         Prevents mixing v1 environmental records with v2 kinematic coaching attestations.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (uint256) pure returns (struct ProgressAttestation.ProgressRecord memory[] memory)

Project","details":"Each device is identified by a `deviceId` derived as keccak256 of its      uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (65 bytes: 0x04 || x || y).      IoTeX supports ECDSA-P256 signature verification via a precompile at      address 0x0100. The EVM does not natively support P256, so all on-chain      signature checks MUST use this precompile. See {PoACVerifier} for usage.      Registration requires a deposit to prevent Sybil attacks. The deposit is      held for the lifetime of the device registration and can be reclaimed      upon deactivation after a cooldown period.      Integrates with IoTeX ioID: the deviceId concept maps to the ioID      decentralized identity, enabling cross-protocol device lookup.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"canClaimBounty(bytes32)":{"details":"Base implementation: any active device is eligible. The deposit is locked for DEACTIVATION_COOLDOWN before withdrawal.","params":{"_deviceId":"The device to deactivate."}},"getDeviceInfo(bytes32)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of pubkey)."},"returns":{"info":"The DeviceInfo struct."}},"getDevicePubkey(bytes32)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier."},"returns":{"_0":"The uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (65 bytes)."}},"getReputationScore(bytes32)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier."},"returns":{"score":"Reputation score in [0, MAX_REPUTATION] (basis points)."}},"isDeviceActive(bytes32)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier."},"returns":{"_0":"True if the device exists and is active."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"registerDevice(bytes)":{"details":"The caller becomes the device owner. A deposit >= minimumDeposit must      be sent with the transaction. The deviceId is derived as      keccak256(pubkey) and must not already be registered.      The pubkey must be a 65-byte uncompressed SEC1 point (prefix 0x04).      Actual P256 curve-point validation is deferred to the first PoAC      verification via the IoTeX P256 precompile (0x0100).","params":{"_pubkey":"Uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (65 bytes)."},"returns":{"deviceId":"The derived device identifier."}},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"setMinimumDeposit(uint256)":{"params":{"_newMinimum":"New minimum deposit in wei."}},"setReputationUpdater(address,bool)":{"params":{"_authorized":"True to grant, false to revoke.","_updater":"Address to grant/revoke."}},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"updateReputation(bytes32,uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"details":"Called by authorized updaters (PoACVerifier, BountyMarket) after      verification events. The reputation score is computed as:          base = verifiedPoACCount (capped contribution)          bonus = corroborationCount * 2          penalty = disputeCount * 10          score = clamp((base + bonus - penalty) * MAX_REPUTATION / normalization, 0, MAX_REPUTATION)      This is a simple initial model; governance can upgrade the formula.","params":{"_addCorroborations":"Number of new corroborations to add.","_addDisputes":"Number of new disputes to add.","_addVerified":"Number of newly verified PoACs to add.","_deviceId":"Target device."}},"withdrawDeposit(bytes32)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"The deactivated device whose deposit to withdraw."}}},"title":"DeviceRegistry","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"DepositWithdrawn(bytes32,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when deposit is withdrawn after deactivation + cooldown."},"DeviceDeactivated(bytes32,address,uint64)":{"notice":"Emitted when a device is deactivated by its owner."},"DeviceRegistered(bytes32,address,uint256,uint64)":{"notice":"Emitted when a new device is registered."},"ReputationUpdated(bytes32,uint16,uint16,uint32,uint32,uint32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a device's reputation score is updated."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"DEACTIVATION_COOLDOWN()":{"notice":"Cooldown period after deactivation before deposit can be withdrawn."},"MAX_REPUTATION()":{"notice":"Maximum value for the reputation score (basis-point scale, 10000 = 100%)."},"PUBKEY_LENGTH()":{"notice":"Length of an uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (0x04 || x || y)."},"canClaimBounty(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns true if the device is eligible to claim bounty rewards."},"computeDeviceId(bytes)":{"notice":"Derive the deviceId from a public key without registering."},"deactivateDevice(bytes32)":{"notice":"Deactivate a registered device. Project","details":"Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.      Pipeline:        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):        Offset  Field                  Size        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B        0x60    world_model_hash       32B        0x80    inference_result       1B        0x81    action_code            1B        0x82    confidence             1B        0x83    battery_pct            1B        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)        ---     total                  164B      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"constructor":{"params":{"_deviceRegistry":"Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.","_maxTimestampSkew":"Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds."}},"getRecordSchema(bytes32)":{"params":{"_recordHash":"SHA-256 hash of the PoAC body."},"returns":{"isSet":"        False when record was verified via legacy verifyPoAC() call.","schemaVersion":"1 = v1 environmental, 2 = v2 kinematic. 0 if not set."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of device pubkey).","_rawBody":"The 164-byte serialized PoAC body (exactly as signed).","_signature":"ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s, 64 bytes)."},"returns":{"recordHash":"SHA-256 of the body (matches firmware chain head)."}},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"details":"Records that fail are silently skipped (not reverted).      The BatchVerified event reports counts."},"verifyPoACWithSchema(bytes32,bytes,bytes,uint8)":{"details":"Identical to verifyPoAC() but additionally persists the schema version so that         downstream contracts (ProgressAttestation) can enforce schema compatibility.","params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of device pubkey).","_rawBody":"The 164-byte serialized PoAC body (exactly as signed).","_schemaVersion":"Sensor schema: 1 = v1 environmental, 2 = v2 kinematic. Must be > 0.","_signature":"ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s, 64 bytes)."},"returns":{"recordHash":"   SHA-256 of the body (matches firmware chain head)."}}},"stateVariables":{"submittedHashes":{"details":"Unlike {verifiedRecords} (set only on full verification success),         submittedHashes is set in the OUTER batch context so it persists even         when the child verification call reverts, permanently preventing         re-submission of any body that entered a batch \u2014 regardless of outcome."}},"title":"PoACVerifier","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"BatchVerified(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a batch verification completes."},"PoACVerified(bytes32,bytes32,uint32,int64,uint8,uint8,uint32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"P256_PRECOMPILE()":{"notice":"IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address."},"POAC_BODY_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature)."},"POAC_SIG_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s)."},"deviceRegistry()":{"notice":"Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract."},"getRecordSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns the sensor commitment schema version for a verified record."},"maxTimestampSkew()":{"notice":"Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds)."},"recordHasSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"True if a schema version was explicitly provided for this record hash.         Distinguishes \"schema not set\" (false) from \"schema 0 explicitly set\" (impossible \u2014         verifyPoACWithSchema rejects schemaVersion==0)."},"recordInferences(bytes32)":{"notice":"Inference result extracted from each verified PoAC body at byte offset 0x80.         Key insight: _parseBody() already parses this; we persist it for downstream queries.         Default 0 for unverified records (cheat codes start at 0x28, so 0 is always clean)."},"recordSchemas(bytes32)":{"notice":"Sensor commitment schema version for records verified via verifyPoACWithSchema().         0 = not set / unknown (record submitted via legacy verifyPoAC()).         1 = v1 environmental (Pebble Tracker: temp/humidity/pressure/GPS/accel).         2 = v2 kinematic/haptic (DualShock: sticks/triggers/gyro/accel + adaptive trigger)."},"submittedHashes(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this body was ever submitted to this contract."},"totalVerifiedCount()":{"notice":"Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices."},"verifiedRecords(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Verify a single PoAC record from its raw wire-format bytes."},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"notice":"Batch-verify multiple PoAC records."},"verifyPoACExternal(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Not for direct use."},"verifyPoACWithSchema(bytes32,bytes,bytes,uint8)":{"notice":"Verify a single PoAC record and record its sensor commitment schema version."}},"notice":"On-chain verification of Project","details":"Stores cryptographic attestations of measurable skill improvement,      backed by verified PoAC records. Coaching platforms can query these      proofs to verify student improvement before issuing certificates,      badges, or processing refund guarantees.      Workflow:        1. Student plays with VAPI device, generating PoAC chain        2. Agent detects improvement (e.g., reaction_time decreased 15%)        3. Coach or student calls attestProgress() with baseline + current records        4. Contract verifies both records exist on-chain        5. Progress attestation stored with metric type and improvement BPS        6. Coaching platform queries getProgressHistory() for verification      Metric types:        REACTION_TIME   \u2014 Average reaction time improvement        ACCURACY        \u2014 Stick/aim precision improvement        CONSISTENCY     \u2014 Reduced timing variance        COMBO_EXECUTION \u2014 Complex input sequence mastery","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"attestProgress(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,uint8,uint32)":{"params":{"_baselineHash":"SHA-256 hash of the baseline (pre-coaching) PoAC body.","_currentHash":"SHA-256 hash of the current (post-coaching) PoAC body.","_deviceId":"Device that generated both records.","_improvementBps":"Improvement magnitude in basis points (100 = 1%).","_metricType":"Which skill metric improved."},"returns":{"attestationId":"   Unique ID for this attestation."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
